Description: Number of direct collaboration partners for a given organization
Interpretation_High: Actively collaborating with many organizations; hub organization
Interpretation_Low: Few direct collaborations; peripheral or specialized organization
Use_Case: Identify most active collaborators and potential hubs

Description: Proportion of possible collaborations that are realized (normalized degree)
Interpretation_High: Highly connected relative to network size; central player
Interpretation_Low: Weakly connected; marginal player in the network
Use_Case: Find central organizations for stakeholder engagement

Description: Frequency with which a partner lies on shortest paths between others; measures brokerage role
Interpretation_High: Acts as bridge/broker between different groups; controls information flow
Interpretation_Low: Not a bridge; collaborates within own group; limited brokerage
Use_Case: Identify brokers that could facilitate cross-sector collaboration

Description: Inverse of average distance to all other partners; measures reach efficiency
Interpretation_High: Can efficiently reach entire network; good position for spreading information
Interpretation_Low: Far from others on average; limited reach; isolated position
Use_Case: Find partners best positioned to disseminate knowledge/technology

Description: Influence based on connections to other influential partners; measures prestige
Interpretation_High: Connected to other prestigious/influential organizations; elite status
Interpretation_Low: Connected to less influential partners; not in elite circle
Use_Case: Identify prestigious partners for credibility and influence

Description: Proportion of actual edges vs. all possible edges in the network; measures overall interconnectedness
Interpretation_High: Highly interconnected network; many collaboration opportunities
Interpretation_Low: Sparse network; few collaboration ties; fragmented ecosystem
Use_Case: Assess overall network health and collaboration culture

Description: Probability that a partner's collaborators also collaborate with each other; measures local cohesion
Interpretation_High: Tight-knit collaboration groups; partners form closed triangles
Interpretation_Low: Partners' collaborators don't work together; star-like structures
Use_Case: Identify tight-knit groups and ecosystem pockets

Description: Proportion of closed triangles in the network; measures global tendency to cluster
Interpretation_High: Network has strong tendency to cluster; cohesive ecosystem
Interpretation_Low: Few closed triangles; more tree-like or fragmented structure
Use_Case: Measure overall network cohesion and trust indicators

Description: Strength of division into communities (0-1); higher means stronger community structure
Interpretation_High: Distinct collaboration communities exist; segmented network
Interpretation_Low: Weakly defined communities; more homogeneous collaboration patterns
Use_Case: Understand if network is divided by sector, region, or ownership

Description: Longest shortest path between any two partners; measures network span
Interpretation_High: Large network with distant parts; may have peripheral groups
Interpretation_Low: Compact network; all partners relatively close
Use_Case: Assess whether network has disconnected peripheral groups

Description: Average number of steps between any two partners; measures compactness
Interpretation_High: Indirect collaborations require many intermediaries; less efficient access
Interpretation_Low: Direct or one-step collaborations common; efficient network
Use_Case: Evaluate ease of collaboration and knowledge transfer
